help.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about identity verification

general.title: General Questions
general.q1: What is the National Identity Verification Portal?
general.a1: The National Identity Verification Portal is an official Government of India initiative that provides secure digital identity verification services to citizens. It enables paperless verification of identity documents through a simple online process.
general.a2: Any Indian citizen with valid identity documents (Aadhaar Card and PAN Card) can use this service. You must be 18 years or older and have access to a device with a camera for biometric verification.
general.q3: Is this service free of charge?
general.a3: Yes, the identity verification service is completely free for all citizens. There are no hidden charges or fees associated with the verification process.
general.q4: How long does the verification process take?
general.a4: Most applications are processed within 24-48 hours. The automated verification happens instantly, while officer review may take 1-2 business days.

documents.title: Document Requirements
documents.q1: What documents do I need for verification?
documents.a1: You need two documents: (1) Aadhaar Card - front side clearly visible, and (2) PAN Card - front side clearly visible. Both documents should be original and not expired.
documents.q2: What format should my document images be in?
documents.a2: Document images should be in JPG or PNG format, with a maximum file size of 5MB each. Ensure the images are clear, well-lit, and all text is readable.
documents.q3: Can I use scanned copies of my documents?
documents.a3: Yes, you can use scanned copies as long as they are clear and all details are legible. Photographs taken with a smartphone camera are also acceptable.
documents.q4: What if my document image is rejected?
documents.a4: If your document is rejected, you will receive a notification explaining the reason. Common issues include blurry images, partial visibility, or glare. You can resubmit with a clearer image.

biometric.title: Biometric Verification
biometric.a1: Biometric face verification captures your face from multiple angles (front, left, right, up) to confirm your identity and ensure you are physically present during verification.
biometric.q2: Why do I need to capture my face from 4 angles?
biometric.a2: Multi-angle capture provides more accurate verification and helps prevent fraud. It ensures the system can confirm your identity from different perspectives.
biometric.q3: What if my camera is not working?
biometric.a3: You need a working camera to complete verification. If your device camera is not working, try using a different device such as a smartphone or laptop with a functional camera.
biometric.q4: Tips for a successful face capture?
biometric.a4: Ensure good lighting on your face, remove glasses or face coverings, look directly at the camera, keep a neutral expression, and ensure your full face is visible.

account.title: Account & Login
account.q1: How do I create an account?
account.a1: Click on Login/Register and select the Register tab. Enter your full name, email address, and create a secure password. You will receive a confirmation once your account is created.
account.q2: I forgot my password. How can I reset it?
account.a2: On the login page, click Forgot Password. Enter your registered email address and follow the instructions sent to your email to reset your password.
account.q3: Can I change my registered email address?
account.a3: Currently, email address changes are not supported through self-service. Please contact our support team with valid identification to request an email change.
account.a4: Yes, all account information is encrypted and stored securely following government data protection guidelines. We use industry-standard security measures.

status.title: Application Status
status.q1: How can I check my application status?
status.a1: After logging in, go to Dashboard and click on View Status or navigate directly to the Application Status page to see your verification status.
status.q2: What do the different status messages mean?
status.a2: Submitted means received, Under Automated Verification means AI checks in progress, Under Officer Review means human review pending, Approved means verification complete, Rejected means additional information may be required.
status.q3: My application has been pending for a long time. What should I do?
status.a3: Most applications are processed within 48 hours. If your application has been pending for more than 3 business days, please contact our support team with your application ID.
status.q4: Can I withdraw or cancel my application?
status.a4: Once submitted, applications cannot be withdrawn as they enter the verification queue. If you made an error, you may need to wait for rejection and then resubmit with correct information.

security.title: Privacy & Security
security.q1: How is my personal data protected?
security.a1: Your data is protected using encryption both in transit and at rest. We follow the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and IT Act, 2000 guidelines for data security.
security.q2: Who has access to my documents and information?
security.a2: Only authorized government officers involved in the verification process have access to your data. Access is logged and audited for security compliance.
security.q3: How long is my data stored?
security.a3: Verification data is stored as per government retention policies, typically for the validity period of the verification plus a mandatory retention period as per applicable laws.
security.q4: Can I request deletion of my data?
security.a4: Data deletion requests are handled as per the Digital Personal Data Protection Act. You can submit a request through the support channel.

guidelines.title: Important Guidelines
guidelines.do2: Ensure good lighting for face capture
guidelines.do4: Keep your application ID for reference
guidelines.dont1: Don't upload blurry or cropped documents
